findings.title: Missing mitigation: Signature enforcement (FORCE_INTEGRITY)
findings.description: Signature enforcement (FORCE_INTEGRITY) is not enabled. Driver can load without a valid Authenticode signature.

findings.title: Missing mitigation: Control Flow Guard (/guard:cf)
findings.description: Control Flow Guard (/guard:cf) is not enabled. Indirect calls unvalidated — control-flow hijacking easier.

findings.title: Driver is NOT HVCI-compatible
findings.description: Driver fails HVCI checks (missing FORCE_INTEGRITY or has W+X sections). Cannot load in HVCI-enabled environments without policy bypass.

findings.title: Kernel write gadgets found (6 instances)
findings.description: Multiple 'mov [reg+offset], reg' patterns in driver code. If the register is derived from user input (e.g., IOCTL buffer), this is an arbitrary kernel memory write primitive.

details.gadgets: 0x140001013: mov qword ptr [rax + 8], rbx; 0x140001017: mov qword ptr [rax + 0x10], rbp; 0x14000101B: mov qword ptr [rax + 0x18], rsi; 0x14000101F: mov qword ptr [rax + 0x20], rdi

findings.title: Hidden function with purpose 'file write' at 0x140086960
findings.description: Function at 0x140086960 performs 'file write' but is not reachable via the normal DriverEntry → MajorFunction call graph.  It may be invoked via a timer, DPC, work item, or kernel callback — a hidden secondary attack path.

findings.title: No PreviousMode / RequestorMode check detected
findings.description: Driver does not import ExGetPreviousMode or KeGetPreviousMode and no inline KTHREAD.PreviousMode read was found. Without checking PreviousMode, a kernel-mode caller could be impersonated, or user-mode requests may bypass validation. IOCTLs should verify RequestorMode == UserMode before trusting input.

findings.title: IOCTL handlers missing InputBufferLength validation
findings.description: The following IOCTL handlers do not appear to validate InputBufferLength before processing the buffer: 0xBAD0B0B0. Without size checks, a short buffer could cause pool overread or out-of-bounds writes.

findings.title: IOCTL handlers missing NULL buffer check
findings.description: These IOCTL handlers may not check for NULL SystemBuffer before dereferencing: 0xBAD0B0B0. A zero-length IOCTL request can result in SystemBuffer=NULL, causing a kernel NULL-pointer dereference (BSOD).

findings.title: No privilege check APIs imported
findings.description: Driver handles IOCTLs but does not import SeSinglePrivilegeCheck, SePrivilegeCheck, or SeAccessCheck. Sensitive operations should verify caller privileges beyond just device handle access rights.

findings.title: IoCreateDevice without FILE_DEVICE_SECURE_OPEN
findings.description: Driver uses IoCreateDevice instead of IoCreateDeviceSecure and FILE_DEVICE_SECURE_OPEN flag was not detected. Without this flag, the device object inherits permissive default security, allowing any user to open a handle. Use IoCreateDeviceSecure with an SDDL string to restrict access.

findings.title: No SDDL security descriptor found
findings.description: No SDDL security descriptor string detected in the binary. Drivers should apply SDDL-based ACLs to their device objects to control which users/groups can open handles.

findings.title: Driver has Authenticode signature
findings.description: PE contains a WIN_CERTIFICATE structure with embedded signature.

findings.title: Signed by: HackSysTeam
findings.description:
Subject: HackSysTeam (HackSys Team)
Issuer: HackSysTeam
Serial: 4e872cb53821eca8434e8c0dbb692947
Key: RSA 2048-bit
Valid until: 2029-06-28T15:19:31+00:00

findings.title: Driver signed with SELF-SIGNED certificate
findings.description: The signing certificate is self-signed (issuer == subject). Self-signed drivers have no trust chain and cannot be verified by the OS certificate store. This requires test-signing mode to be enabled, which is a significant security downgrade. Malware often uses self-signed certs.

findings.title: No timestamp countersignature detected
findings.description: The Authenticode signature has no countersignature timestamp. Without a timestamp, the signature becomes invalid after the signing certificate expires. Timestamped signatures remain valid indefinitely. This affects driver loadability.

findings.title: PKCS#7 signature verifies cryptographically
findings.description: The primary Authenticode PKCS#7 signature was verified against the signer certificate's public key.

findings.title: Authenticode PE hash matches (binary not tampered)
findings.description: Recomputed sha1 hash of the PE image matches the value embedded in SpcIndirectDataContent.

findings.title: Chain does not terminate at a Windows kernel-trusted root
findings.description: The certificate chain does not chain up to any root present in the Windows kernel trust store (20 known roots checked by thumbprint). Unless a missing intermediate can be supplied by the OS at load time, Windows will not accept this driver.

findings.title: Load restricted: Self-signed certificate
findings.description: This driver is self-signed. It will only load on Windows with test-signing mode enabled (bcdedit /set testsigning on). A watermark appears on the desktop when test-signing is active.

findings.title: HVCI incompatible: missing FORCE_INTEGRITY flag
findings.description: This driver is NOT compatible with Hypervisor-protected Code Integrity (HVCI / Memory Integrity). On systems with HVCI enabled, Windows will BLOCK this driver from loading. Issues: missing FORCE_INTEGRITY flag.

findings.title: Driver architecture: x64
findings.description: 64-bit driver — loads on x64 Windows

findings.title: Load compatibility: BLOCKED — 4 issue(s) prevent loading
findings.description:
[BLOCKED] Kernel Trust Anchor: Chain does not terminate at a Windows kernel trust root (no-ms-root)
  [BLOCKED] Self-Signed: Self-signed drivers require test-signing mode
  [BLOCKED] HVCI: Blocked when Memory Integrity is ON (missing FORCE_INTEGRITY flag)
  [BLOCKED] Secure Boot: Blocked when Secure Boot is enabled

blockers: Primary signature is SHA-1, timestamped after 2015-07-29 cutoff, and no modern nested signature — rejected on Win10 1903+
Primary signature is SHA-1, timestamped after 2015-07-29 cutoff, and no modern nested signature — rejected on Win10 1903+
Primary signature is SHA-1, timestamped after 2015-07-29 cutoff, and no modern nested signature — rejected on Win10 1903+
FORCE_INTEGRITY flag not set (HVCI requires it on the PE header)

passes: Any valid signature accepted under test-signing

blockers: No WHQL attestation EKU — S Mode / Secured-Core require WHQL-signed drivers
No Authenticode page hashes (Secured-Core requires per-page integrity)

findings.title: Device Access Security Audit
findings.description: Uses IoCreateDevice (not IoCreateDeviceSecure); Missing FILE_DEVICE_SECURE_OPEN flag; Device is not exclusive (multiple handles allowed); Exposed via symlinks: \DosDevices\HackSysExtremeVulnerableDriver, \??\C:\Windows\System32\HEVD.log; No SDDL security descriptor — default permissive ACL

findings.title: ROP/JOP gadgets found: 610 total (misc: 260, reg-control: 146, memory-read: 79, stack-pivot: 70, memory-write: 45, jmp-reg: 10)
findings.description:
Found 610 usable ROP/JOP gadgets in executable sections. Since many drivers lack ASLR, these gadgets are at predictable addresses and can be chained for kernel exploitation.

Top gadgets by type:
  [stack-pivot] (70 found):
    0x1400010EC: leave ; call 0x140001110 ; add rsp, 0x28 ; ret
    0x1400013A6: leave ; dec r8 ; mov byte ptr [rcx], al ; jne 0x1400013a0 ; mov rax, r11 ; ret
    0x1400015D9: leave ; jne 0x1400015d0 ; and r8, 0xf ; je 0x1400015e8 ; movups xmmword ptr [rcx + r8 - 0x10], xmm0 ; ret
  [memory-write] (45 found):
    0x140001209: mov byte ptr [rcx - 1], al ; jne 0x140001200 ; mov rax, r11 ; ret
    0x140001207: dec eax ; mov byte ptr [rcx - 1], al ; jne 0x140001200 ; mov rax, r11 ; ret
    0x140001206: dec r8 ; mov byte ptr [rcx - 1], al ; jne 0x140001200 ; mov rax, r11 ; ret
  [memory-read] (79 found):
    0x14000108A: mov edi, dword ptr [rsp + 0x48] ; add rsp, 0x20 ; pop r14 ; ret
    0x140001089: mov rdi, qword ptr [rsp + 0x48] ; add rsp, 0x20 ; pop r14 ; ret
    0x140001088: mov rdi, qword ptr [rsp + 0x48] ; add rsp, 0x20 ; pop r14 ; ret
  [reg-control] (146 found):
    0x140001093: pop rsi ; ret
    0x140001092: pop r14 ; ret
    0x14000108F: add esp, 0x20 ; pop r14 ; ret
  [jmp-reg] (10 found):
    0x14000117E: add byte ptr [rax], al ; jmp rax
    0x14000117C: add byte ptr [rax], al ; add byte ptr [rax], al ; jmp rax
    0x14000117A: test byte ptr [rax], al ; add byte ptr [rax], al ; add byte ptr [rax], al ; jmp rax
